phases.name: Walking skeleton — the counter-thread on GET /claims/{cid}

steps.name: Wire the read-only query_counter_claims read + pure CounterThread ADT + thread render into GET /claims/{cid} and return the htmx detail fragment with the thread (THICK walking skeleton)
steps.description: The thick foundational walking-skeleton step (US-CT-001 capability + US-CT-002 render, CT-1). It forces the WHOLE counter-thread thread into existence end-to-end. (ports) Add `query_counter_claims(&self, target_cid: &str) -> Result<Vec<CounterClaimRow>, StoreReadError>` to the read-only StoreReadPort trait + the flat `CounterClaimRow` boundary DTO (non-Option author_did + cid, Option<String> reason, f64 confidence, DateTime<Utc> composed_at, PeerOrigin origin) — NO mutation method added to the port (I-CT-1). (adapter-duckdb) Impl it on DuckDbStoreReadAdapter (src/store_read.rs) as the ADR-046 2-step read: Step A = the indexed UNION ALL ref lookup over the SAME shared connection (claims JOIN claim_references + peer_claims JOIN peer_claim_references, WHERE referenced_cid = ? AND ref_type = 'counters', explicit author_did + cid + source_table discriminant, ORDER BY composed_at, source_table, cid — intra-store JOINs only, no cross-store merge/AVG/GROUP-BY); Step B = a per-row read_artifact_at(artifact_path) to decode each counter's unsigned.reason (Option<String>) — LOCAL only, no network; returns Ok(vec![]) for an un-countered target. (viewer-domain) Add the PURE CounterThread ADT (None | Countered { counters: Vec<CounterEntry> }) + the CounterEntry view-model + the total projection Vec<CounterClaimRow> -> CounterThread (empty -> None; non-empty -> Countered, one entry per row, order preserved, is_own derived from PeerOrigin), and EXTEND render_claim_detail_fragment to render the counter-thread BENEATH the existing render_claim_fields + render_evidence_section (naming each counter's author_did + its CID + verbatim reason, reusing render_confidence single-site + the (you) annotation + the /claims/{counter_cid} link). (adapter-http-viewer) In claim_detail_page (src/lib.rs), on the Ok(Some(detail)) arm, also call store.query_counter_claims(cid), project to CounterThread, and pass it to the extended render — reading the store the ViewerServer ALREADY holds (no new field), Shape fork reused UNCHANGED, no new route, no network. Materialize the seed_claim_with_counter support seam (via seed_peer_authored_graph for Rachel's 0.91 target + the production `claim counter --reason` verb for Maria's OWN counter) and the assert_counter_thread_renders_attributed_verbatim helper body in support/mod.rs. BUILD-BEFORE-RUN: the AT spawns the openlore (viewer) bin via ViewerServer::start, so the run MUST cargo build the bin before executing these ATs. NO second binary (the 2-step read is a LOCAL read).
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} with the HX-Request header, over a seeded local store where Rachel's claim (confidence 0.91) is countered by Maria's own counter (seed_claim_with_counter), returns 200 text/html containing ONLY the #claim-detail fragment (no page chrome, no <head>): the claim renders verbatim (confidence 0.91) and, beneath it, a counter-thread section names the counter's author DID, its own CID, and its verbatim reason.

StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method query_counter_claims(&str) -> Result<Vec<CounterClaimRow>, StoreReadError> + the CounterClaimRow DTO (non-Option author_did + cid, Option reason); NO mutation method is added to the port; the DuckDbStoreReadAdapter implements it as the ADR-046 2-step read (indexed UNION ALL ref lookup over the shared connection filtered to ref_type='counters' with explicit author_did + cid + no cross-store merge JOIN, then a per-row artifact read for the reason), LOCAL only with no network call, returning an empty vec for an un-countered CID.

The detail route reads the store the ViewerServer ALREADY holds (no new field), holds NO signing key, and adds no write/sign/counter route; the counter's CID renders only as render-only navigation TEXT toward /claims/{counter_cid}.
The original claim's confidence renders through the single render_confidence site (0.91 verbatim, never 0.9 or 91%), unchanged by the presence of the counter.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} with the HX-Request header, over a seeded local store where Rachel's claim (confidence 0.91) is countered by Maria's own counter (seed_claim_with_counter), returns 200 text/html containing ONLY the #claim-detail fragment (no page chrome, no <head>): the claim renders verbatim (confidence 0.91) and, beneath it, a counter-thread section names the counter's author DID, its own CID, and its verbatim reason.

StoreReadPort gains a single read-only method query_counter_claims(&str) -> Result<Vec<CounterClaimRow>, StoreReadError> + the CounterClaimRow DTO (non-Option author_did + cid, Option reason); NO mutation method is added to the port; the DuckDbStoreReadAdapter implements it as the ADR-046 2-step read (indexed UNION ALL ref lookup over the shared connection filtered to ref_type='counters' with explicit author_did + cid + no cross-store merge JOIN, then a per-row artifact read for the reason), LOCAL only with no network call, returning an empty vec for an un-countered CID.

The detail route reads the store the ViewerServer ALREADY holds (no new field), holds NO signing key, and adds no write/sign/counter route; the counter's CID renders only as render-only navigation TEXT toward /claims/{counter_cid}.
The original claim's confidence renders through the single render_confidence site (0.91 verbatim, never 0.9 or 91%), unchanged by the presence of the counter.
steps.mutation_note: Adds the load-bearing pure-core surface (CounterThread ADT + the Vec<CounterClaimRow> -> CounterThread projection + the thread render beneath the claim) AND the ADR-046 2-step adapter read. Extend nightly mutation testing to this projection/read (empty -> None vs non-empty -> Countered branch, per-counter author_did + cid + verbatim reason attribution, ref_type='counters' filter, the no-cross-store-merge UNION ALL, and the per-row artifact reason decode).

steps.name: GET /claims/{cid} no-JS full page + fragment parity (page embeds the same detail fragment fn, thread included)
steps.description: Complete the detail render contract for the counter-thread (CT-2; I-CT-6). GET /claims/{cid} WITHOUT HX-Request serves a COMPLETE full page (chrome + the SAME #claim-detail region with the claim + counter-thread) for the no-JS no-regression path; the #claim-detail region inside the full page is structurally IDENTICAL to the htmx fragment — parity by construction because the full-page render EMBEDS render_claim_detail_fragment (the thread is rendered INSIDE the fragment fn the page embeds). Confirmatory once 01-01 exists (no new read; the thread already renders inside the fragment fn — this pins that the full page embeds it). The full page is the contract; the htmx swap is a nicety.
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a countered claim (seed_claim_with_counter) WITHOUT HX-Request returns a complete full HTML page (is_full_page(): <head>, page chrome, and the embedded #claim-detail region); WITH HX-Request returns the bare #claim-detail fragment (is_fragment(), no chrome).

The counter's author DID, its CID, and its verbatim reason appear in BOTH the full-page body and the htmx-fragment body — the full page embeds the same detail fragment fn (parity by construction, I-CT-6).
No divergence between the two shapes: the same claim (confidence 0.91 verbatim) and the same counter-thread render in both.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a countered claim (seed_claim_with_counter) WITHOUT HX-Request returns a complete full HTML page (is_full_page(): <head>, page chrome, and the embedded #claim-detail region); WITH HX-Request returns the bare #claim-detail fragment (is_fragment(), no chrome).

The counter's author DID, its CID, and its verbatim reason appear in BOTH the full-page body and the htmx-fragment body — the full page embeds the same detail fragment fn (parity by construction, I-CT-6).
No divergence between the two shapes: the same claim (confidence 0.91 verbatim) and the same counter-thread render in both.

phases.name: Attribution + verbatim reason + the empty-reason edge (REAL work)

steps.name: The counter reason renders verbatim and its CID is a one-hop drill-link (depth-1, no recursion)
steps.description: The verbatim-reason + drill-link scenario for US-CT-002 (CT-5; I-CT-3 / I-CT-4 / ADR-047). The counter's free-text --reason renders EXACTLY as authored (including punctuation), the original claim's confidence renders verbatim (0.91, never 0.9/91%), and the counter's CID is a render-only <a href> to /claims/{counter_cid} — a ONE-HOP drill into the counter's own detail, with NO nested/recursive counter render (depth-1, ADR-047). Reuses seed_claim_with_counter + assert_counter_thread_renders_attributed_verbatim from 01-01; pins the verbatim reason byte-fidelity and the depth-1 drill-link href on the observable surface.
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a countered claim (seed_claim_with_counter capturing the verbatim reason + the counter CID) renders the counter's reason text byte-for-byte (including its punctuation), the original claim's confidence as 0.91 verbatim (never 0.9 or 91%), and the counter's CID as an <a href="/claims/{counter_cid}"> render-only one-hop drill-link.
The drill-link is depth-1: the counter's own counters are NOT rendered inline (no nested/recursive thread); drilling renders the counter's detail via the same /claims/{cid} route.
Each counter CID is render-only navigation TEXT (an <a href> anchor), never an executable control.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a countered claim (seed_claim_with_counter capturing the verbatim reason + the counter CID) renders the counter's reason text byte-for-byte (including its punctuation), the original claim's confidence as 0.91 verbatim (never 0.9 or 91%), and the counter's CID as an <a href="/claims/{counter_cid}"> render-only one-hop drill-link.
The drill-link is depth-1: the counter's own counters are NOT rendered inline (no nested/recursive thread); drilling renders the counter's detail via the same /claims/{cid} route.
Each counter CID is render-only navigation TEXT (an <a href> anchor), never an executable control.
steps.mutation_note: The verbatim-reason render + the depth-1 /claims/{counter_cid} href (the no-nested-render boundary) are load-bearing; mutation-test the verbatim reason rendering and the drill-link href construction so a mutant that truncates/escapes the reason or that recurses into nested counters is killed.

steps.name: Two counters by distinct authors render as two attributed items, never merged (anti-merging — needs the new peer-counter seed builder, REAL work)
steps.description: The CARDINAL anti-merging GOLD scenario for the counter-thread (CT-4; I-CT-3 / KPI-AV-2 / KPI-GRAPH-2 / KPI-FED-1). A claim countered by TWO DISTINCT authors — Maria's OWN counter via `claim counter` (-> claims) AND peer Tobias's counter via `peer add` + `peer pull` (-> peer_claims) — renders TWO attributed counter entries under their OWN author DIDs + CIDs + verbatim reasons, and NEVER a merged 'disputed by 2' / consensus aggregate row. REAL work: the existing build_verifiable_peer_records_for_triples hardcodes references: Vec::new(), so this step MUST add a new peer-counter seed builder (e.g. build_verifiable_peer_counter_record(tobias_did, seed, target_cid, reason)) that emits a verifiable peer signed record carrying references:[{type:counters, cid:target_cid}] + a reason, so the peer counter is produced (and CID-recomputed + verified) by the production pull pipeline — NOT hand-inserted. Materialize seed_claim_two_counters_distinct_authors + the assert_counter_thread_two_attributed_no_merge helper body in support/mod.rs. The CounterThread ADT has NO aggregate variant by construction (ADR-047), and the adapter UNION ALL projects author_did + cid explicitly with no cross-store merge JOIN (ADR-046).
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a claim countered by two distinct authors (seed_claim_two_counters_distinct_authors: Maria's own counter + peer Tobias's counter) renders EXACTLY two attributed counter entries — one naming Maria's DID + her CID, one naming Tobias's DID + his CID — each with its own verbatim reason.
No single row aggregates the two counters into a 'disputed by 2' / consensus / net-verdict row on the rendered surface (anti-merging; the entries are never collapsed across authors).

The peer counter is produced by the production federation pull path from a verifiable peer record carrying references:[{type:counters, cid:target_cid}] (the new peer-counter seed builder), not a hand-inserted store row; the read returns it via the indexed UNION ALL over claims and peer_claims with explicit per-author attribution.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a claim countered by two distinct authors (seed_claim_two_counters_distinct_authors: Maria's own counter + peer Tobias's counter) renders EXACTLY two attributed counter entries — one naming Maria's DID + her CID, one naming Tobias's DID + his CID — each with its own verbatim reason.
No single row aggregates the two counters into a 'disputed by 2' / consensus / net-verdict row on the rendered surface (anti-merging; the entries are never collapsed across authors).

The peer counter is produced by the production federation pull path from a verifiable peer record carrying references:[{type:counters, cid:target_cid}] (the new peer-counter seed builder), not a hand-inserted store row; the read returns it via the indexed UNION ALL over claims and peer_claims with explicit per-author attribution.
steps.mutation_note: The per-counter no-merge projection (one CounterEntry per (author, cid), NO aggregate variant in the ADT) plus the new peer-counter seed builder are load-bearing anti-merging logic; mutation-test the two-entries-never-one-entry branch and the per-entry author_did + cid rendering so a mutant that merges or elides an author is killed.

steps.name: A counter with no reason renders an explicit 'no reason provided' state (empty-reason edge, REAL work)
steps.description: The empty-reason edge for US-CT-002 (CT-6; ADR-047 / ADR-015 wire-optional). A counter whose reason is absent (a peer record authored by a non-OpenLore client, unsigned.reason == None) renders an explicit 'no reason provided' state — the counter's author DID + its CID are STILL shown — never a blank line, never a crash. The empty-reason edge is total at the type level (reason: Option<String> in CounterClaimRow/CounterEntry). REAL work: requires the new peer-counter seed builder (from 02-02) in its reason-OMITTED form to land a verifiable peer counter with no reason via `peer add` + `peer pull`, plus the render arm that maps None/empty-after-trim -> 'no reason provided'. Materialize seed_counter_empty_reason + the assert_counter_thread_empty_reason_state helper body in support/mod.rs.
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a claim countered by a peer record whose reason is absent (seed_counter_empty_reason) renders the counter entry with its author DID + its CID STILL shown, AND an explicit 'no reason provided' state in place of the reason text.
The empty-reason counter never renders a blank line and never causes a 5xx / panic (the edge is total via reason: Option<String>; ADR-047).

The peer counter with no reason is produced by the production federation pull path from a verifiable peer record (the reason-OMITTED form of the peer-counter seed builder), not a hand-inserted store row.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a claim countered by a peer record whose reason is absent (seed_counter_empty_reason) renders the counter entry with its author DID + its CID STILL shown, AND an explicit 'no reason provided' state in place of the reason text.
The empty-reason counter never renders a blank line and never causes a 5xx / panic (the edge is total via reason: Option<String>; ADR-047).

The peer counter with no reason is produced by the production federation pull path from a verifiable peer record (the reason-OMITTED form of the peer-counter seed builder), not a hand-inserted store row.
steps.mutation_note: The empty-reason render arm (None/empty-after-trim -> 'no reason provided', author DID + CID still shown) is load-bearing reliability logic; mutation-test the Some(reason) vs None branch so a mutant that renders a blank line or drops the attribution is killed.

phases.name: No-noise discipline + neutral presence flag + 404 no-regression

steps.name: An un-countered claim shows no counter section and no empty noise; the countered claim is shown verbatim never re-weighted (no-noise + shown-never-applied)
steps.description: Two story-local scenarios that ride the same no-noise / shown-never-applied discipline (CT-7 no-noise + CT-3 shown-never-applied; US-CT-003 / US-CT-002, I-CT-2). (CT-7) An un-countered claim (seed_uncountered_claim) yields an empty query_counter_claims result -> CounterThread::None -> the detail renders the claim ALONE: NO 'Counter-claims' section, NO 'Countered' flag, NO '0 counters' / 'no disagreement' empty-state noise (byte-unaffected common case). (CT-3) The SAME claim (confidence 0.91) rendered un-countered (seed_uncountered_claim) and countered (seed_claim_with_counter) shows the claim's confidence + fields byte-identical in both — the counter is additive context BELOW, never a re-weight ABOVE. Materialize seed_uncountered_claim + the assert_no_counter_thread_noise + assert_counter_claim_verbatim_unchanged helper bodies in support/mod.rs. Confirmatory once 01-01 exists (the None arm + verbatim render already present); pins the no-noise + shown-never-applied properties on the observable surface.
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over an un-countered claim (seed_uncountered_claim) renders the claim + evidence as in slice-06 (status 200) with NO 'Counter-claims' section, NO 'Countered' presence flag, and NO '0 counters' / 'no disagreement' empty-state text.

The SAME claim rendered un-countered and countered shows its confidence (0.91 verbatim) and its claim-region fields byte-identical in both renders — the counter never re-weights, filters, merges, or re-ranks the claim (shown-never-applied, I-CT-2 / I-CT-4).
assert_no_counter_thread_noise and assert_counter_claim_verbatim_unchanged are materialized in support/mod.rs and assert on the observable rendered HTML only.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over an un-countered claim (seed_uncountered_claim) renders the claim + evidence as in slice-06 (status 200) with NO 'Counter-claims' section, NO 'Countered' presence flag, and NO '0 counters' / 'no disagreement' empty-state text.

The SAME claim rendered un-countered and countered shows its confidence (0.91 verbatim) and its claim-region fields byte-identical in both renders — the counter never re-weights, filters, merges, or re-ranks the claim (shown-never-applied, I-CT-2 / I-CT-4).
assert_no_counter_thread_noise and assert_counter_claim_verbatim_unchanged are materialized in support/mod.rs and assert on the observable rendered HTML only.
steps.mutation_note: The None arm (no section, no flag, no empty-state noise) and the shown-never-applied verbatim render are load-bearing; mutation-test the empty -> None no-noise branch and the confidence/fields-unchanged property so a mutant that emits '0 counters' noise or re-weights the claim is killed.

steps.name: A countered claim shows a neutral 'Countered' presence flag, not a verdict
steps.description: The neutral presence-flag scenario for US-CT-003 (CT-8; I-CT-3). A countered claim (seed_claim_with_counter) shows a neutral 'Countered' presence indicator near the claim — a PRESENCE marker only, NEVER a verdict, a score, a count ('disputed by N'), or a count-based re-rank. The flag makes disagreement legible without picking a winner. Reuses seed_claim_with_counter; adds the neutral presence-flag render on the Countered arm + materializes the assert_counter_thread_presence_flag_is_neutral helper body in support/mod.rs. Largely confirmatory once 01-01's Countered arm exists; pins the neutral (non-verdict, non-count) phrasing of the flag.
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a countered claim (seed_claim_with_counter) marks the claim with a neutral 'Countered' presence flag (presence only).
No verdict / score / 'disputed by N' count-based phrasing appears anywhere on the rendered surface — the flag is a marker, never a weight, count, or re-rank (I-CT-3).
assert_counter_thread_presence_flag_is_neutral is materialized in support/mod.rs and asserts the neutral presence flag is present AND no count/verdict phrasing is present.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} over a countered claim (seed_claim_with_counter) marks the claim with a neutral 'Countered' presence flag (presence only).
No verdict / score / 'disputed by N' count-based phrasing appears anywhere on the rendered surface — the flag is a marker, never a weight, count, or re-rank (I-CT-3).
assert_counter_thread_presence_flag_is_neutral is materialized in support/mod.rs and asserts the neutral presence flag is present AND no count/verdict phrasing is present.
steps.mutation_note: The neutral presence-flag render (presence-only, no count/verdict phrasing) is load-bearing; mutation-test the flag-present branch and the no-count phrasing so a mutant that emits a 'disputed by N' count or a verdict is killed.

steps.name: An unknown CID keeps the existing guided not-found with no thread added (no slice-06 regression)
steps.description: The 404 boundary for US-CT-003 (CT-9; slice-06 V-7 no-regression). A CID not in the store STILL shows the EXISTING slice-06 guided not-found page ('No claim with that identifier in your store' + a back link to /claims) — UNCHANGED by slice-11. The 404 path gains NO counter thread / flag (the thread is built ONLY on the Ok(Some(detail)) arm, never the not-found arm). Confirmatory once 01-01 exists (the thread is wired into the found arm only); pins that slice-11 does not regress slice-06 V-7. Reuses seed_claim_with_counter (so the store is non-empty) + the existing not-found assertions.
criteria: GET /claims/{cid} for a CID that is NOT in the store (with the store non-empty via seed_claim_with_counter) renders the EXISTING slice-06 guided not-found message 'No claim with that identifier in your store' + a '/claims' back link, UNCHANGED.
The 404 body carries NO 'Counter-claims' section and NO 'Countered' presence flag — the thread is built only on the claim-found arm (no slice-06 V-7 regression).
The not-found path is read-only and adds no write/sign/counter control.
acceptance_criteria: GET /claims/{cid} for a CID that is NOT in the store (with the store non-empty via seed_claim_with_counter) renders the EXISTING slice-06 guided not-found message 'No claim with that identifier in your store' + a '/claims' back link, UNCHANGED.
The 404 body carries NO 'Counter-claims' section and NO 'Countered' presence flag — the thread is built only on the claim-found arm (no slice-06 V-7 regression).
The not-found path is read-only and adds no write/sign/counter control.

phases.name: Gold guardrails — read-only, no-write, offline, shown-never-applied (cross-cutting, LAST)

steps.name: Every detail route with counters leaves the store read-only + no detail response adds a write/sign control (gold)
steps.description: Two cross-cutting gold guardrails that tend to flip green for free once Phases 01-03 are done (CT-INV-ReadOnly + CT-INV-NoWrite; I-CT-1 / KPI-VIEW-2). (ReadOnly) Exercising the detail route across postures (countered + un-countered) AND both shapes (full page + htmx fragment), plus a non-existent CID, leaves the claims + peer_claims row counts UNCHANGED, asserted via the inherited universe-bound assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe (Mandate 8: the two port-exposed counts) — over a NON-TRIVIAL store (own + peer counter via seed_claim_two_counters_distinct_authors so the read-only delta is over a populated store). The thread is computed per query and persists nothing. (NoWrite) No detail response shape (full page or fragment, countered or not) renders a sign/publish/counter/subscribe control; any /claims/{cid} drill-link present is render-only <a href> navigation TEXT. Both reuse the inherited harnesses (assert_store_read_only, capture_store_row_count_universe) + assert_detail_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control (materialize its body in support/mod.rs). Lands in viewer_counter_claim_threads_invariants.rs.
criteria: Exercising the detail route (countered + un-countered CID + a non-existent CID, full page + htmx fragment) over a non-trivial store leaves the claims and peer_claims row counts identical (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe, all slots unchanged) — any change is an UNSHIPPABLE write-surface breach (I-CT-1).
The counter-thread is recomputed per query and never persisted (zero new persisted rows; the read-only universe is the inherited port-exposed counts, Mandate 8).

No detail response shape (full page or fragment, countered or un-countered) renders a sign/publish/counter/subscribe control (assert_detail_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control over EVERY shape x posture).

Any /claims/{cid} drill-link present is render-only navigation TEXT (an <a href> anchor), never an executable control; assert_detail_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control is materialized in support/mod.rs.
acceptance_criteria: Exercising the detail route (countered + un-countered CID + a non-existent CID, full page + htmx fragment) over a non-trivial store leaves the claims and peer_claims row counts identical (assert_store_read_only over capture_store_row_count_universe, all slots unchanged) — any change is an UNSHIPPABLE write-surface breach (I-CT-1).
The counter-thread is recomputed per query and never persisted (zero new persisted rows; the read-only universe is the inherited port-exposed counts, Mandate 8).

No detail response shape (full page or fragment, countered or un-countered) renders a sign/publish/counter/subscribe control (assert_detail_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control over EVERY shape x posture).

Any /claims/{cid} drill-link present is render-only navigation TEXT (an <a href> anchor), never an executable control; assert_detail_html_has_no_write_or_sign_control is materialized in support/mod.rs.

steps.name: The counter-thread chrome stays offline (no CDN) and the whole thread renders fully offline (gold)
steps.description: Two offline gold guardrails (CT-INV-OfflineChrome + CT-INV-Offline; I-CT-5 / KPI-HX-G2 and I-CT-5 / KPI-5). (OfflineChrome) The countered-claim detail full page references ONLY the LOCAL /static/htmx.min.js script src and NO off-host CDN (references_external_cdn() is false in both shapes). (Offline) The counter-thread renders fully with NO network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start) over a store seeded with a PULLED PEER counter (verified at pull time) — the 2-step read (indexed DB ref lookup + local artifact fs::read for the reason) is LOCAL with NO outbound edge to take down, so the peer counter's author DID + CID + verbatim reason render with no Unavailable/degraded notice in both shapes (offline-STRONGER than /search; the viewer re-verifies nothing). Reuses seed_claim_with_counter (peer-counter posture) + the slice-06 references_external_cdn + the network-disabled viewer posture. Lands in viewer_counter_claim_threads_invariants.rs; flips green for free once 01-01 + the chrome exist.
criteria: The countered-claim detail full page's only <script src> is the local /static/htmx.min.js; references_external_cdn() returns false for both the full-page and htmx-fragment shapes (no CDN / off-host reference, I-CT-5 / KPI-HX-G2).

GET /claims/{cid} over a claim with a pulled PEER counter, with NO network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start), renders the full counter-thread (the peer counter's author DID + CID + verbatim reason) in BOTH shapes from the LOCAL store with no network call — the 2-step read is LOCAL.
Neither shape renders a network-degraded notice (none of 'unavailable', 'network error', 'could not reach', 'try again') — the counter-thread route has no outbound edge (I-CT-5 / KPI-5).
acceptance_criteria: The countered-claim detail full page's only <script src> is the local /static/htmx.min.js; references_external_cdn() returns false for both the full-page and htmx-fragment shapes (no CDN / off-host reference, I-CT-5 / KPI-HX-G2).

GET /claims/{cid} over a claim with a pulled PEER counter, with NO network seam wired (plain ViewerServer::start), renders the full counter-thread (the peer counter's author DID + CID + verbatim reason) in BOTH shapes from the LOCAL store with no network call — the 2-step read is LOCAL.
Neither shape renders a network-degraded notice (none of 'unavailable', 'network error', 'could not reach', 'try again') — the counter-thread route has no outbound edge (I-CT-5 / KPI-5).

steps.name: SHOWN-NEVER-APPLIED GOLD — the countered claim confidence is byte-identical with and without counters, in both shapes (LAST)
steps.description: The CARDINAL shown-never-applied gold guardrail (CT-INV-ShownNeverApplied; I-CT-2 / OD-AV-7 / ADR-015 — the load-bearing slice-11 invariant; release-blocking). The SAME claim (confidence 0.91) rendered un-countered (seed_uncountered_claim) and countered (seed_claim_with_counter, own + peer counters) shows its rendered confidence AND its claim-region fields byte-IDENTICAL whether or not it is countered — the counter never filters, merges, down-weights, or re-ranks the claim. Disagreement is additive context BELOW the claim and changes NOTHING above. A shown-never-applied regression silently lets a counter mutate the claim being read; this gold makes it unshippable. Generalizes the 03-01 story-local shown-never-applied check to the cross-cutting release gate. Reuses assert_counter_claim_verbatim_unchanged (materialized in 03-01) over both the un-countered and countered renders. LAST roadmap step (it tends to flip green for free once 01-01 + 03-01 hold, because the claim is built from get_claim UNCHANGED and the thread is a separate ADT rendered below).
criteria: The SAME claim (confidence 0.91) rendered un-countered (seed_uncountered_claim) and countered (seed_claim_with_counter) shows its confidence as 0.91 verbatim AND its claim-region fields byte-identical across both renders — the counter never re-weights, filters, merges, or re-ranks the claim (shown-never-applied, I-CT-2 / OD-AV-7 / ADR-015).

The byte-identity holds because the countered claim is built from get_claim UNCHANGED and the counter-thread is a separate ADT rendered BELOW — no code path feeds a counter back into the claim's confidence/fields.

Any divergence in the claim region between the un-countered and countered renders is an UNSHIPPABLE shown-never-applied breach (release-blocking); assert_counter_claim_verbatim_unchanged is asserted over both renders.
acceptance_criteria: The SAME claim (confidence 0.91) rendered un-countered (seed_uncountered_claim) and countered (seed_claim_with_counter) shows its confidence as 0.91 verbatim AND its claim-region fields byte-identical across both renders — the counter never re-weights, filters, merges, or re-ranks the claim (shown-never-applied, I-CT-2 / OD-AV-7 / ADR-015).

The byte-identity holds because the countered claim is built from get_claim UNCHANGED and the counter-thread is a separate ADT rendered BELOW — no code path feeds a counter back into the claim's confidence/fields.

Any divergence in the claim region between the un-countered and countered renders is an UNSHIPPABLE shown-never-applied breach (release-blocking); assert_counter_claim_verbatim_unchanged is asserted over both renders.
steps.mutation_note: The CARDINAL release-blocking shown-never-applied gate. Mutation-test the claim-region byte-identity across countered/un-countered — a mutant that feeds a counter back into the claim's confidence/fields (a re-weight, filter, or re-rank) must be killed (the I-CT-2 gate toward the Phase-5 mutation threshold).

validation.note: APPROVED 0 blockers; 14/14 scenarios covered; real-work isolated
